settings.support_heading: Support continuity near the buy box
settings.support_copy: This product module is structured for compatibility notes, installation guidance, compare-entry points, and support escalation without overloading the purchase flow.

settings.title: Core capability
settings.copy: Use this for a primary technical or workflow benefit that helps the shopper understand the product quickly.

settings.title: Deployment fit
settings.copy: Use this for setup context, device compatibility, or workspace fit that reduces uncertainty before purchase.

settings.title: Support readiness
settings.copy: Use this for downloads, manuals, and after-purchase continuity so support feels integrated rather than bolted on.

settings.heading: A modular layer for feature explanation and technical positioning
settings.copy: Use this layer to move the customer from product interest into product confidence before they commit.

settings.title: Primary feature explanation
settings.copy: Use this panel for the lead product feature and why it matters in a real setup.

settings.title: Workflow or setup value
settings.copy: Use this panel for deployment, platform support, or connection context that helps the shopper self-qualify.

settings.title: Support continuity
settings.copy: Use this panel for firmware, manuals, compatibility reassurance, or long-term ownership confidence.

settings.heading: Specifications
settings.copy: This section is designed to evolve into a metafield-backed technical specification table.
settings.source_note: Use structured rows for final technical data while keeping the fallback table reviewable during content seeding.

settings.heading: A comparison module ready for structured datasets
settings.copy: Use this area to differentiate adjacent products more clearly while keeping compare tied to support and compatibility context.
settings.source_note: Use blocks for early review, then migrate repeated comparison data into structured groups without changing the customer-facing pattern.

settings.title: Comparison candidate
settings.copy: Use this card for an alternate product or adjacent solution with a clear reason to choose it.

settings.title: Comparison candidate
settings.copy: Use this card for a differentiated workflow, port mix, or power profile.

settings.title: Comparison candidate
settings.copy: Use this card for higher-tier, lower-tier, or specialized alternative guidance.

settings.heading: Support questions
settings.copy: Keep product-level questions here when they directly support purchase confidence or setup clarity.

settings.question: Where should product-level support questions live?
settings.answer: Use this section for questions tightly related to setup, usage, or compatibility. Broader support content should move into the support center as structured resources.

settings.question: How will specifications scale later?
settings.answer: The current table is block-based for review speed, but the section is intended to migrate to product metafields or metaobject-backed technical datasets.

settings.heading: Support and compatibility can stay connected to the PDP
settings.copy: Use this module to route from product education into manuals, downloads, compatibility, or direct help without losing the shopping context.
